gates.name: protocol conformance
gates.detail: random: 100/100 self-games, 0 protocol errors / timeouts / illegal moves.

gates.name: warm-up isolation
gates.detail: NPS flat across moves 1-20; no move-1 cold-start spike.

gates.name: language tax (miniature)
gates.detail: cpp-alphabeta vs py-alphabeta (same algorithm): score share 55% fixed-node -> 72% wall-clock. Speed ratio 11.4x (1,682,630 vs 147,905 nps).

markets.label: cpp-alphabeta wins the Fixed-Node Duel
markets.desc: Same algorithm, two languages. Resolves YES if cpp-alphabeta scores over 50% here. (Fixed-node should be a coin-flip; wall-clock shouldn't.)

markets.label: cpp-alphabeta wins the Wall-Clock Duel
markets.desc: Same algorithm, two languages. Resolves YES if cpp-alphabeta scores over 50% here. (Fixed-node should be a coin-flip; wall-clock shouldn't.)

markets.label: The language tax is real: cpp-alphabeta pulls further ahead under wall-clock
markets.desc: cpp-alphabeta and py-alphabeta run the SAME alpha-beta over the SAME eval. Resolves YES if cpp-alphabeta's score share is higher at equal time than at equal nodes — i.e. py-alphabeta loses purely to speed.

markets.label: py-alphabeta out-scores py-mcts in the Group Stage
markets.desc: Knowledge pole vs knowledge pole, same language — which paradigm banks more points across the round robin?

markets.label: random finishes bottom of the Group Stage
markets.desc: Does the protocol canary anchor the table, as an Elo anchor should?
